lessons_learned: The shift of project execution into national hands is increasingly seen as a mechanism for strengthening national ownership as well as providing strong elements of an exit strategy. Selecting the most appropriate institutional counterpart(s) is challenging. Involving actors that develop as well as implement government policy, together with including mechanisms and pathways to allow for adaptive management, would enhance the potential to sustain relevance and gain traction within settings that are dynamic and evolving.

Given the power of gender equality to create the foundation for a peaceful, prosperous, and sustainable world, institutions involved in nation-building are well-placed to demonstrate and drive gender mainstreaming as a permeating value "baked into every decision, at every level".

Striking the 'right' balance between direction and support is key to institution-building remits, which increasingly characterize international development cooperation activities. Situational Leadership Theory provides useful guidance for identifying criteria and transition points to shift amongst styles.
recommendations: Recognize the asset and potential of the RECPC built up through this project's support and determine the ways in which this Centre can be used as a strategic tool for influencing the course of industrial development towards a clean and prosperous Ukraine.
Embrace the RECPC's inherent nature as a Competency Centre, with the accompanying justifications and implications for assuring its continuation.
Explicitly search for opportunities to use the Centre and work with the RECPC as a privileged partner.

Adopt a country strategy to improve coordination across projects and donors operating in Ukraine with the aim of harnessing synergies to enhance impact and to build understanding of the RECPC's capacities and ways in which the Centre could contribute as a partner to others' missions and goals.

Enhance the staff mix to assure expertise and capabilities on the team for delivering all aspects involved in fulfilling the RECPC's vision and mission, notably with respect to policy advice and financial mechanisms, where there are current shortfalls.

Strategically leverage the RECP capability pool to achieve catalytic effect by identifying individuals and organisations in target sectors that could and would act as RECP references, ambassadors and advocates to create a snowball effect to build up mass and momentum for this innovation to Ukrainian industrial practice, based on insights on diffusion of innovation.
executive_summary: This terminal evaluation assessed a UNIDO project implemented in Ukraine from 2011 to 2020 that established and operationalized a Resource Efficient and Cleaner Production Centre (RECPC). The project aimed to promote sustainable industrial practices by providing technical assistance, training, and advisory services to Ukrainian enterprises. The Centre successfully delivered RECP assessments to over 200 companies, generating significant environmental and economic benefits including reduced energy consumption, water use, and waste generation. Key achievements included establishing a financially sustainable business model with the Centre achieving 70% self-financing by project end, developing regional offices to expand geographic reach, and building national capacity through training programs. The evaluation found the project highly relevant to Ukraine's industrial modernization needs and effective in delivering outputs, though sustainability faced challenges due to political instability, economic crises, and the ongoing conflict in eastern Ukraine. The Centre demonstrated resilience by adapting its service delivery model and diversifying revenue streams. Critical success factors included strong partnerships with industry associations, flexible project management, and committed local staff. Recommendations emphasized continued institutional support, enhanced marketing strategies, and strengthening the Centre's role in national policy dialogue on resource efficiency.
thematic_justification: The project focused on Resource Efficient and Cleaner Production (RECP) methodologies that reduced energy consumption, water use, material inputs, and waste generation across industrial enterprises through technical assessments and implementation support.

sdg_mapping.7: The project directly contributed to SDG 7 by implementing energy efficiency measures in over 200 enterprises, resulting in documented reductions in energy consumption and promoting affordable and clean energy access in industrial operations.
sdg_mapping.9: The project supported SDG 9 by strengthening Ukraine's industrial infrastructure through capacity building, technology transfer, and promoting sustainable industrialization practices that enhanced competitiveness while reducing environmental impact.
sdg_mapping.12: The project advanced SDG 12 by promoting responsible production and consumption patterns through RECP methodologies that optimized resource use, minimized waste generation, and encouraged circular economy principles in manufacturing sectors.
sdg_mapping.13: The project contributed to SDG 13 by reducing greenhouse gas emissions through energy efficiency improvements and cleaner production practices that decreased the carbon footprint of participating industrial enterprises.

context.title: Independent Terminal Evaluation: Promoting the Adaptation and Adoption of RECP (Resource Efficient and Cleaner Production) Through the Establishment and Operation of a Cleaner Production Centre (CPC) in Ukraine
